title: En Passant
summary: The pawn capture most players never learn

steps.title: A Sneaky Rule
steps.text: When pawns advance two squares from their starting rank, they can sometimes be captured by an enemy pawn 'in passing.' This is called en passant — French for 'in passing.' It only works on the very next move.

steps.title: The Opportunity
steps.text: Black just played ...f5, zipping two squares past White's e5 pawn. White's pawn could have captured at f6 if the Black pawn had only moved one square. En passant says: you can still do it — just this turn.

steps.prompt: Capture the f5 pawn en passant.
steps.hint: The move is exf6 — your pawn captures as if the f-pawn were on f6.

steps.title: One-Shot Rule
steps.text: You MUST use en passant on the very next move or lose the right. It's one of the most commonly forgotten rules — now you know.
